steps.title: Identify the appositive structure
steps.content: The blank separates the noun nation from the proper noun South Korea, which is an appositive that identifies which nation is meant.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content: A restrictive appositive (essential to identifying the noun) takes no punctuation. Nation South Korea with no punctuation is the correct form. The comma in C marks it non-restrictive, the colon in A introduces elaboration, and the dash in D adds emphasis—none are standard for identifying appositives. That matches choice B — B is correct.
